purpose:
To align the model's predictions with actual business objectives by implementing a cost-sensitive learning approach.
The notebook calculates the optimal probability decision threshold to minimize the total financial impact of false negatives (missed churners) versus false positives (wasted retention efforts).
key_steps: Load the augmented training data and original testing data.
Train a standard LightGBM classifier to generate prediction probabilities.
Define a business-specific cost matrix: $5000 penalty for a False Negative (customer churns) and $500 penalty for a False Positive (unnecessary retention discount).
Iterate through a range of probability thresholds (0.01 to 0.99) to calculate the total theoretical cost at each point.
Identify the optimal threshold that yields the lowest total financial cost.
Apply the optimized threshold to the test set predictions to recalculate standard metrics (Recall, Precision, Accuracy).
Export the results, confusion matrix, and cost optimization curve.

images.05_Cost_Sensitive_Churn_Model_phase2_cos: A line graph plotting total business cost against varying decision thresholds. It highlights a distinct global minimum cost at a highly sensitive threshold of 0.06.
images.05_Cost_Sensitive_Churn_Model_phase2_Cos: A confusion matrix based on the new 0.06 threshold. It shows a massive increase in true positives and false positives, reflecting the model's new aggressive stance on predicting churn.

05_Cost_Sensitive_Churn_Model_phase2_cos.Optimized_LightGBM: Optimal_Threshold: 0.060, Minimum_Cost: 730500, Recall: 0.948, F1: 0.420, Precision: 0.270, ROC-AUC: 0.844, Accuracy: 0.469

key_findings: Because the cost of missing a churner ($5000) is ten times higher than wrongly targeting a loyal customer ($500), the optimal decision threshold dropped drastically from the default 0.50 down to 0.06.
Applying this business-aligned threshold skyrocketed the model's Recall to ~94.8%, ensuring almost no potential churners slip through the cracks.
This aggressive approach sacrifices overall Accuracy (dropping to ~46.9%) and Precision (dropping to ~27.0%) to achieve the lowest possible total monetary loss ($730,500).
notes: This phase represents a crucial shift from optimizing purely statistical metrics (like ROC-AUC) to optimizing for direct ROI. The resulting model acts essentially as an ultra-sensitive radar, casting a very wide net.
